title: Stereoscopic 360 degree digital camera systems
abstract: Stereoscopic 360Â° video recording devices are adapted to facilitate capture of stereoscopic 360Â° images. According to one example, a stereoscopic 360Â° video recording device may include an enclosure with three sides of equal length in a triangular configuration. Six digital camera slots may be included within the enclosure, with each of the three sides including two digital camera slots. A fan may be coupled to a surface of the enclosure to facilitate cooling of digital cameras retained within the enclosure. A digital video camera may be secured within each of the digital camera slots, wherein each digital video camera includes a predefined interaxial distance from a respective digital video camera on its left and a respective digital video camera on its right. Other aspects, embodiments, and features are also included.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A stereoscopic 360Â° video recording device, comprising: an enclosure including three sides of equal lengths in a triangular configuration; six digital camera slots within the enclosure, wherein each of the three sides includes two digital camera slots; and a fan coupled to a surface of the enclosure. 2. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording device of claim 1, wherein each of the six digital camera slots are positioned at equal distances from adjacent digital camera slots on both the right and left sides. 3. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording device of claim 1, further comprising at least one microphone aperture associated with each digital camera slot. 4. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording device of claim 1, further comprising: at least one respective airflow passageway formed on an inside surface of each digital camera slot, wherein each airflow passageway is located and shaped to direct air across one or more surfaces of a digital video camera when the fan is on and when a digital camera is positioned within each respective digital camera slot. 5. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording device of claim 1, wherein the enclosure comprises: a top component; and a base removably coupled to the top component to form the six digital camera slots configured to at least partially enclose a digital camera when positioned within each respective digital camera slot. 6. A stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system, comprising: an enclosure including three sides forming a triangular configuration, each side including two digital camera slots; and 

 a digital video camera secured within each of the digital camera slots, wherein each digital video camera includes an interaxial distance between 62 millimeters and 100 millimeters from a respective digital video camera on its left and a respective digital video camera on its right, and wherein each digital video camera is secured at a zero degree pitch angle. 7. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system of claim 6, further comprising a fan coupled to a surface of the enclosure. 8. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system of claim 7, further comprising a plurality of airflow passageways, wherein each airflow passageway extends from an opening within the enclosure along an inside surface of a respective digital camera slot over at least one surface of a respective digital video camera. 9. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system of claim 6, further comprising a microphone aperture associated with each digital camera slot, each microphone aperture comprising: a vertical surface at least substantially parallel to a respective side forming the triangular configuration an arcuate surface; and an arcuate surface intersecting the vertical surface and extending arcuately downward from the intersection with the vertical surface. 10. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system of claim 6, wherein each digital video camera includes an ultra-wide-angle lens of 220Â° or greater. 11. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system of claim 6, wherein the interaxial distance between each digital video camera is the same. 12. The stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system of claim 6, wherein: the interaxial distance between digital video cameras positioned on a same side of the triangular configuration is a first distance; and 

 the interaxial distance between adjacent digital video cameras positioned on different sides of the triangular configuration is a second distance, wherein the second distance is greater than the first distance. 13. A method of capturing 360Â° digital images, comprising: positioning six digital video cameras in a triangular configuration, with two digital video cameras on each side, wherein each digital video camera is positioned with an interaxial distance between 62 millimeters and 100 millimeters from adjacent digital video cameras, and with a pitch of zero degrees; and simultaneously capturing images on each digital video camera. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: capturing sound in a microphone associated with each digital video camera through a microphone aperture comprising: a vertical surface; and an arcuate surface intersecting the vertical surface and extending arcuately downward from the intersection with the vertical surface. 15. The method of claim 13, further comprising: forcing air across one or more surfaces of each of the digital video cameras. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein simultaneously capturing images on each digital video camera comprises simultaneously capturing images on each digital video camera employing an ultra- wide- angle lens of at least 220Â°. 17. A method of making a stereoscopic 360Â° video recording system, comprising: forming an enclosure with three lateral sides of equal lengths in a triangular configuration; forming six digital camera slots within the enclosure, wherein two video camera slots are positioned on each lateral side of the enclosure; and coupling a fan to a surface of the enclosure. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising: 

 forming at least one airflow passageway on an inner surface of each digital camera slot, wherein each airflow passageway is located and shaped to direct air across one or more surfaces of a digital video camera when the fan is on and when a digital camera is positioned within a digital camera slot. 19. The method of claim 17, further comprising: forming a microphone aperture associated with each digital camera slot, each microphone aperture including an arcuate surface intersecting a vertical surface and extending arcuately downward from the intersection with the vertical surface. 20. The method of claim 17, further comprising: disposing a digital video camera within each of the digital camera slots, wherein each digital video camera is positioned with an interaxial distance between 62 millimeters and 100 millimeters from adjacent digital video cameras, and with a pitch of zero degrees.
